narrative.headline: 2022 GMC Sierra 1500: engine problems dominate owner complaints
narrative.summary: Across 523 owner complaints to NHTSA, the engine is by far the most-reported area at 210 reports (40%) — more than the next two categories combined. Power train (96 reports, 18%) and structure (91 reports, 17%) follow, then the electrical system (78 reports, 15%) and service brakes (29 reports, 6%). Most complaints did not involve a crash, fire, or injury. The few that did are spread across categories: 3 fires each in the engine and electrical system reports, 3 crashes in the power train reports, and one injury each in power train, structure, and electrical.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Most-reported area by far: 210 complaints (40%), including 3 fires and 1 crash, with no injuries reported. Owners describe a loud knocking noise from the engine, in some cases on a nearly new truck.

issues.sample: On 05/28/2022 my family was driving my brand new 2022 GMC Sierra 1500 3.0 Diesel at a rate of 60mph. There was suddenly a loud clunk (transmission) after proceeding a little further and getting speed back to 60mph there was a similar “clunk”… the diesel truck then immediately gai
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 96 complaints (18%), including 3 crashes, 1 fire, and 1 injury — the most crashes of any category here. Owners describe a loud clunk from the transmission while driving at highway speed.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: 91 complaints (17%), including 1 crash and 1 injury, with no fires reported. One owner describes a piece detaching from the front grille while driving.

issues.sample: Truck drove as expected with no prior warnings.  We pulled over for road snacks/drinks upon starting the truck we got a whole slew of warnings and warning lights.  The warnings included:. Service ESC, Service Trailer Brake System, Service Brake Assist, Steering Assist is reduced
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 78 complaints (15%), including 3 fires, 1 crash, and 1 injury. Owners describe a cluster of warning lights and messages appearing at once, such as Service ESC and Service Trailer Brake System.

issues.sample: Truck drove as expected with no prior warnings.  We pulled over for road snacks/drinks upon starting the truck we got a whole slew of warnings and warning lights.  The warnings included:. Service ESC, Service Trailer Brake System, Service Brake Assist, Steering Assist is reduced
issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 29 complaints (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe brake-related warnings such as Service Trailer Brake System and Service Brake Assist appearing among a set of dashboard alerts.
